refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to separate these into distinct sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating different functional requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on 'what' rather than 'how'.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map capturing categorized site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and site feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to downstream functions
Provide an interface for updating and validating the worksite model against operational policies
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and site feature data into a unified worksite model accessible to downstream functions

potential_sources: Tight coupling between terrain representation and site feature categorization modules
Dependency of planning/execution functions on specific data formats or update mechanisms
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a unified worksite model DP supports independence by encapsulating terrain and site features together, reducing cross-module dependencies. Care must be taken to define clear interfaces to avoid coupling with downstream functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data sources
Risk of model complexity impacting update performance and accessibility
Potential challenges in aligning model representation with diverse planning/execution function needs
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain representation and site feature categorization into distinct DPs if future requirements indicate significant differences in update frequency or data sources, to enhance modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses data processing and integration functions related to the worksite environment, including terrain and site feature data management.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw terrain and site feature data from sensors and external sources.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Provides the unified worksite model to downstream functions for task planning and execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and remote components.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain and site data integration is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data is centralized here.
Vehicle module uses integrated data but does not perform integration; would cause diffusion if integration logic placed here.
Teleoperation consumes integrated data but is not responsible for integration; poor containment.
Safety Services may use integrated data for alerts but is not involved in integration; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it logically centralizes the integration of terrain and site feature data into a unified worksite model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on external data sources may require careful interface management to avoid data inconsistencies.
If integration logic grows too complex, risk of module becoming a bottleneck or overly large.
Downstream modules must rely on timely updates from NGHE Solution, requiring robust interface protocols.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically satisfies the FR by specifying the integration of terrain and site feature data into a unified worksite model, which directly addresses the requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has high cohesion and minimal diffusion scores. Integration logic is centralized, minimizing diffusion risk. Other modules consume the integrated data but do not perform integration, which supports clear ownership and containment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces a unified worksite model accessible to downstream planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution module clearly aligns with the FR and DP, providing a clear allocation of responsibility for implementation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the complexity growth of the NGHE Solution module to avoid it becoming a bottleneck or overly large.
Ensure robust interface protocols and timely updates to downstream modules to maintain data consistency and usability.
Manage dependencies on external data sources carefully to prevent data inconsistencies and integration issues.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well satisfied by the design parameter and implementation module. The DP logically satisfies the FR, the DP is well contained within a cohesive implementation module with minimal diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to avoid unnecessary decomposition that could complicate verification and increase risk. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring complexity and managing interfaces rather than further decomposition.

revise.why: Monitor the complexity growth of the NGHE Solution module to avoid it becoming a bottleneck or overly large, and ensure robust interface protocols and timely updates to maintain data consistency and usability.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can handle the integration complexity without performance degradation.
Downstream modules will reliably consume the unified worksite model as intended.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth in the NGHE Solution module leading to maintenance challenges.
Dependency on external data sources may cause data inconsistencies or integration issues.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the NGHE Solution module produces the unified worksite model as specified.
Interface test results ensuring timely and consistent data updates to downstream modules.
